role_hints.role: Appraisers, inspectors and certification specialists (hypothesized dominant professional core)
role_hints.oews_basis: role_mix is 4-digit 5419 and dominated by veterinary occupations from sibling 541940 (veterinarians 17.1%, vet techs 9.2% of wage bill) — unusable as a proxy for this catch-all; the plausible real occupations (13-2023 Appraisers ex-real-estate, various inspection/technician SOCs, 19-2021 Atmospheric Scientists for weather services) sit outside the listed top-10. Hypothesis structure, flagged as such
role_hints.note: Valuation write-ups, condition reports and certification paperwork automate heavily; physical inspection acts (marine surveys, gem grading in-hand, commodity inspection, equipment appraisal site visits) and credential-backed sign-offs persist.

role_hints.role: Field and technical staff (inspection, testing, site work)
role_hints.oews_basis: not in the contaminated top-10; hypothesis
role_hints.note: Being physically present to inspect a vessel, pipeline weld, consigned lot or livestock — no current AI substitution; drone/imaging tools augment.

role_hints.role: Analysts and specialist professionals (weather forecasting, patent brokerage, consultants in the tail)
role_hints.oews_basis: not in the contaminated top-10; hypothesis
role_hints.note: Commercial weather forecasting and analytical report production are heavily AI-exposed; bespoke advisory relationships persist.

role_hints.oews_basis: 41-3091 Sales Reps of Services 3.3% appears in the contaminated mix; hypothesis ~8-12%
role_hints.note: Quoting and engagement letters automate; referral relationships (lenders, insurers, attorneys ordering appraisals) persist.

role_hints.oews_basis: 11-1021 General and Operations Managers 6.6% of wage bill (plausible signal even in the contaminated mix)
role_hints.note: Scheduling, dispatch and reporting automate; client escalation persists.

role_hints.oews_basis: 43-4051 Customer Service Reps 2.3% + long tail of admin occupations, est. remainder ~15-20%
role_hints.note: Order intake, report delivery, invoicing and license/credential tracking automate near-fully.

source_hints.source: ASA (American Society of Appraisers) — personal property, machinery & equipment, business valuation disciplines
source_hints.why: The credentialing body for non-real-estate appraisal, a hypothesized major sub-segment; member counts and standards (USPAP via The Appraisal Foundation) define the professional gate.

source_hints.source: NAMS / SAMS (National Association of Marine Surveyors / Society of Accredited Marine Surveyors)
source_hints.why: Marine surveying is a named 541990 activity; these bodies evidence credential gates and practitioner counts.

source_hints.source: American Meteorological Society (AMS) commercial weather industry statements and BLS coverage of private weather services
source_hints.why: Commercial weather forecasting sits in this code; AI weather models (GraphCast-class) are a direct product substitution story worth checking.

source_hints.source: BizBuySell Insight Reports (miscellaneous professional services listings)
source_hints.why: Catch-all micro-firms trade as main-street SDE deals; no institutional comp universe exists for the code as a whole.

source_hints.source: USPAP (Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice) and lender/insurer appraisal-ordering requirements
source_hints.why: The mechanism that decides whether appraisal work is credential-gated (bypass-resistant) or AI-substitutable: who is required to order a credentialed appraisal and for what.

capture_questions: Most work here is fixed-fee-per-report or per-inspection (appraisal fees, survey fees, forecast subscriptions) — when AI collapses report-drafting time, do referral sources (lenders, insurers, courts, auction houses) who order at market rates let operators keep the margin, or does fee competition among credentialed providers pass it through?

Who owns the client relationship — the firm, or the individually credentialed professional (ASA appraiser, accredited marine surveyor) whose signature the ordering party requires? What does a roll-up retain when the credential-holder retires?

Which revenue lines are credential/liability-gated against AI bypass (USPAP-compliant appraisals for lending/insurance/litigation, accredited marine surveys for underwriting) versus advisory outputs a client could generate with generic AI (market-value opinions, condition summaries, routine forecasts)?

For the weather-services sub-segment: AI models now outperform traditional numerical forecasting cheaply — does subscription forecasting revenue survive when the underlying capability is commoditized, or does value migrate to decision-integration platforms?
terminal_value_question: A catch-all's terminal value is its dominant sub-segment's: for credential-gated inspection/appraisal work the deciding mechanism is that a third party (lender, insurer, court) requires a liability-bearing signature from an accredited human who physically examined the thing — that survives mature AI; for output-only analytical products (forecasts, desktop valuations, research reports) AI replaces the product outright. What is this code's actual revenue split between signature-plus-site-visit work and deliverable-only analysis — and would a researcher notice that the two halves have opposite terminal values?
special_notes: Catch-all code: 541990 spans appraisal (except real estate), marine surveying, commodity/cargo inspection, weather forecasting, patent brokerage, arbitration-adjacent services and a residual of unclassifiable professional services. Identify the top-3 revenue sub-segments, score the DOMINANT one, and set heterogeneous: true. Hypothesis (flagged): non-real-estate appraisal + inspection/surveying services form the dominant cluster, but verify — the residual nature of this code makes composition genuinely uncertain.

DATA ANOMALY — labor_share 0.8164 is implausibly high for any real industry and likely reflects QCEW/SUSB classification mismatch (QCEW assigns many establishments to 541990 as a residual that SUSB's receipts base does not include). It is not null, so it stands as the dataset input, but sanity-check any V computation against sub-segment reality (professional services labor shares run 0.35-0.55) and flag the discrepancy in top_uncertain_inputs.
Role-mix contamination is total (4-digit 5419 is veterinary-dominated) — the role_hints above are explicit hypotheses; do not cite the vet occupation shares as if they described this code.

EBITDA normalization: 18,002 of 21,690 firms are <5 employees — SDE with owner labor inside; credential-holder key-person risk is the dominant diligence issue (the credential is personal and non-transferable).

Boundary confusion: real-estate appraisal is 531320 (much bigger and often conflated), testing laboratories are 541380, building inspection is 541350, management consulting is 541611, and 'all other' survey data frequently mixes them — check what any benchmark actually covers before applying it here.

Residual-code churn: firms get parked in 541990 by default; year-over-year counts and receipts are noisier than for well-defined codes — prefer multi-year averages over single-year snapshots where possible.
